metric_name: Y2K remediation urgency and scale prediction
metric_value: Y2K represents a once-in-generation technology crisis requiring massive sustained effort; tool selection critical to outcome

metric_name: Y2K scale and cost actual outcome
metric_value: Worldwide Y2K spending totaled approximately $100 billion ($365 per US resident); spending peaked 1998-1999; transition on 1/1/2000 passed without major systemic failures; Aberdeen's scale estimates proved directionally accurate
notes: Verified via US Commerce Dept. report and Wikipedia Y2K article

metric_value: Y2K tool market collapsed after 2000; many vendors went out of business or pivoted; Aberdeen's skepticism about tool vendor claims proved warranted — effectiveness varied widely
